title: pyosys autogeneration: Parser skips a few functions due to line breaks in header
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

* Build yosys with pyosys Python extension
* Run code below:

```
from pyosys import libyosys as ys

def addSignal(m, name, n, public = False):
    w = m.addWire(name, n)
    return ys.SigSpec(w)

def ID(x):
    return ys.IdString("$" + x)

d = ys.Design()
m = d.addModule(ID("test1"))
clk, r, d, q = [ addSignal(m, ID("sig_%d" % i), 1) for i in range(4)]
c = ys.Const(0, 1)
m.addAdff(ID("adff"), clk, r, d, q, c, 0, 0)
```
## Expected behavior

`$adff` Element being added to the module

## Actual behavior

Method not found, because not generated in the python module from the parser auxiliary `misc/py_wrap_generator.py`, due to undetected line breaks in `rtlil.h`

Quick workaround: Remove line breaks. See also patch.

[patch-pyosys.txt](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/4592229/patch-pyosys.txt)
